title.es: Seguro Medico para Contratistas 1099 en 2026 | CoveredUSA

description.en: 1099 contractors: ACA marketplace options, Form 7206 deduction (income tax only, not SE tax), HSA limits, and the 2026 subsidy cliff at 400% FPL.
description.es: Contratistas 1099: opciones del mercado ACA, deduccion Formulario 7206, limites HSA y el tope del subsidio 2026 en 400% FPL.

h1.en: Health Insurance for 1099 Independent Contractors in 2026
h1.es: Seguro Medico para Contratistas Independientes 1099 en 2026

subhero.en: 1099 contractors have no employer paying half the premium, but they do have Form 7206 (a 100% above-the-line deduction) and HSA-qualified HDHPs that W-2 workers cannot access. Here are the real options, the tax math, and the 2026 subsidy cliff you need to plan around.
subhero.es: Los contratistas 1099 no tienen empleador que pague la mitad de la prima, pero tienen el Formulario 7206 (una deduccion del 100% above-the-line) y los HDHP compatibles con HSA. Aqui estan las opciones reales, la matematica fiscal y el tope del subsidio 2026.

quickAnswer.en: 1099 independent contractors and freelancers typically choose between (1) an ACA Marketplace plan with Premium Tax Credits if projected MAGI stays under 400% FPL ($63,840 for a single filer in 2026), (2) an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan at full sticker price paired with a maxed Health Savings Account for the triple tax advantage, or (3) a spouse's employer plan if one is available. The self-employed health insurance deduction on Form 7206 lets independent contractors write off 100% of premiums above the line, reducing federal income tax and MAGI but NOT self-employment tax on Schedule SE. For 2026, the enhanced Premium Tax Credit subsidies from the Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, meaning the 400% FPL cliff is fully back.
quickAnswer.es: Los contratistas independientes 1099 y freelancers tipicamente eligen entre (1) un plan del Mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima si el MAGI proyectado esta bajo 400% FPL ($63,840 para un declarante individual en 2026), (2) un Plan con Deducible Alto compatible con HSA a precio completo con una Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud maximizada, o (3) el plan del empleador del conyuge. La deduccion de seguro medico del Formulario 7206 permite a los contratistas independientes deducir el 100% de las primas above-the-line, reduciendo el impuesto sobre la renta y el MAGI pero NO el impuesto al trabajo independiente en el Schedule SE.

introParagraphs.en: 1099 independent contractors, freelancers, and sole proprietors face a health insurance market that was not designed for them. No employer pays half the premium. No HR department picks the plan. No pretax payroll deduction appears automatically. A contract worker earning $80,000 in 1099-NEC income is responsible for sourcing, buying, and deducting coverage entirely on their own. The good news: independent contractors have access to tax tools that salaried W-2 employees do not, and stacking them correctly can cut effective health insurance costs by 30 to 50 percent.
introParagraphs.es: Los contratistas independientes 1099, freelancers y propietarios unicos enfrentan un mercado de seguros medicos que no fue disenado para ellos. Ningun empleador paga la mitad de la prima. Ningun departamento de RR.HH. elige el plan. Un trabajador por contrato que gana $80,000 en ingresos 1099-NEC es responsable de buscar, comprar y deducir la cobertura por completo. La buena noticia: los contratistas independientes tienen acceso a herramientas fiscales que los empleados W-2 no tienen.

introParagraphs.en: Four real options await 1099 independent contractors in 2026: the ACA Marketplace with Premium Tax Credits, an HSA-qualified HDHP at full price, a spouse's employer plan, and COBRA from a prior W-2 job. The sections below detail the Form 7206 self-employed health insurance deduction (with the Schedule SE caveat that trips up first-year contractors), the HSA triple tax strategy, and the Marketplace Special Enrollment Period triggers that let independent contractors enroll outside open enrollment. Gig-economy drivers who receive 1099-K forms from platforms like Uber, Lyft, or DoorDash should also check the rideshare drivers page for state-specific stipend programs. For contractors with employees, the small business owners page covers SHOP and ICHRA.
introParagraphs.es: Esta guia cubre las cuatro opciones reales para contratistas 1099 en 2026, la deduccion del Formulario 7206 (con la advertencia del Schedule SE), la estrategia triple del HSA, y los desencadenadores del Periodo de Inscripcion Especial del Mercado que permiten a los contratistas independientes inscribirse fuera de la inscripcion abierta.

en: ACA Marketplace with Premium Tax Credits
1099 contractors with MAGI under 400% FPL ($63,840 single)
es: Mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima
Contratistas 1099 con MAGI bajo 400% FPL ($63,840 individual)

en: Independent contractors above the subsidy cliff or seeking triple tax advantage
es: HDHP compatible con HSA a precio completo
Contratistas independientes sobre el tope del subsidio o buscando ventaja fiscal triple

en: Married freelancers with an employed spouse
es: $0 a $400/mes parte del empleado (antes de impuestos)

en: COBRA from a prior W-2 job
Contract workers who recently left employer coverage and are mid-treatment
es: COBRA de un empleo W-2 anterior
Trabajadores por contrato que recientemente dejaron cobertura del empleador y estan en tratamiento

footnote.en: All marketplace premiums shown after the Form 7206 self-employed health insurance deduction. The 2026 subsidy cliff is fully restored at 400% FPL. Above $63,840 single or $132,000 for a household of four, 1099 contractors pay full sticker price. Source: IRS Form 7206, HealthCare.gov, KFF 2026 analysis.
footnote.es: Todas las primas del mercado mostradas despues de la deduccion del Formulario 7206. El tope del subsidio 2026 esta completamente restaurado en 400% FPL. Por encima de $63,840 individual o $132,000 para un hogar de cuatro, los contratistas 1099 pagan el precio completo. Fuente: IRS Formulario 7206, HealthCare.gov, analisis KFF 2026.

heading.en: Option 1: ACA Marketplace Plan with Premium Tax Credits
heading.es: Opcion 1: Plan del Mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima

paragraphs.en: ACA Marketplace plans are the default path for most 1099 independent contractors and freelancers because they combine comprehensive coverage with income-based Premium Tax Credits. For 2026, the critical threshold is 400% of the Federal Poverty Level ($63,840 for a single filer, $132,000 for a household of four). Subsidies phase down as income climbs toward that line and stop entirely at 400% FPL. The Inflation Reduction Act's enhanced credits expired January 1, 2026, restoring the hard cliff that existed before 2021. A contract worker projecting MAGI of $62,000 saves thousands per year; the same worker at $64,000 pays full sticker price.
paragraphs.es: Los planes del Mercado ACA son el camino predeterminado para la mayoria de los contratistas independientes 1099 y freelancers porque combinan cobertura integral con Creditos Fiscales de Prima basados en ingresos. Para 2026, el umbral critico es el 400% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza ($63,840 para un declarante individual, $132,000 para un hogar de cuatro personas). Los subsidios se reducen a medida que los ingresos se acercan a esa linea y se detienen completamente al 400% FPL.

paragraphs.en: MAGI projection is more complex for 1099 contractors than for W-2 workers because it involves multiple deductions stacked in sequence. Start with gross 1099-NEC income, subtract deductible business expenses from Schedule C, subtract half of self-employment tax (Schedule SE line 13), subtract the Form 7206 self-employed health insurance deduction, and subtract any HSA contributions or retirement plan contributions (SEP-IRA, Solo 401(k)). A sole proprietor with $100,000 in gross 1099 income can easily project a MAGI below 400% FPL after these deductions stack. Section 1095-A is the IRS form marketplace enrollees receive in January; use it with Form 8962 to reconcile advance Premium Tax Credits against actual MAGI at tax time.
paragraphs.es: La proyeccion del MAGI es mas compleja para los contratistas 1099 que para los empleados W-2. Empiece con el ingreso bruto 1099-NEC, reste los gastos de negocio deducibles del Schedule C, reste la mitad del impuesto al trabajo independiente, reste la deduccion del Formulario 7206 y reste los aportes al HSA o plan de retiro. Un propietario unico con $100,000 en ingresos brutos 1099 puede facilmente proyectar un MAGI debajo del 400% FPL. El Formulario 1095-A es el formulario del IRS que los inscritos en el mercado reciben en enero; uselo con el Formulario 8962 para conciliar los creditos adelantados.

heading.en: Option 2: HSA-Qualified HDHP at Full Price
heading.es: Opcion 2: HDHP compatible con HSA a precio completo

paragraphs.en: For 1099 independent contractors and self-employed freelancers above the 400% FPL subsidy cliff, an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) is often the highest-return option after taxes. The HDHP must meet the 2026 IRS minimum deductible threshold ($1,700 self-only, $3,400 family) to qualify for Health Savings Account pairing. The sticker premium for an HDHP is typically $200 to $400 per month lower than a comparable Gold or Platinum plan, and every dollar you put into the Health Savings Account is deductible above the line on Schedule 1.
paragraphs.es: Para los contratistas independientes 1099 y freelancers por encima del tope del subsidio del 400% FPL, un Plan con Deducible Alto (HDHP) compatible con HSA es a menudo la opcion de mayor retorno despues de impuestos. El HDHP debe cumplir con el umbral de deducible minimo 2026 del IRS ($1,700 individual, $3,400 familia) para calificar para la Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud. La prima del HDHP es tipicamente $200 a $400 por mes mas baja que un plan Gold o Platinum comparable.

paragraphs.en: The Health Savings Account attached to the HDHP gives contract workers the triple tax advantage: contributions are deductible above the line in 2026 up to $4,400 for self-only coverage or $8,750 for family coverage (plus a $1,000 catch-up if you are 55 or older), HSA funds grow tax-free, and qualified medical withdrawals are tax-free. Unlike a Flexible Spending Account, which is employer-only and not available to sole proprietors or freelancers without W-2 employment, the Health Savings Account is portable and rolls over year to year with no use-it-or-lose-it rule. A Schedule C filer in the 24% federal bracket who maxes the self-only HSA saves roughly $1,056 in federal income tax on the contribution alone.
paragraphs.es: La Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud vinculada al HDHP da a los trabajadores por contrato la ventaja fiscal triple: las contribuciones son deducibles above-the-line en 2026 hasta $4,400 para cobertura individual o $8,750 para cobertura familiar (mas $1,000 adicional a los 55 anos), los fondos del HSA crecen libres de impuestos y los retiros medicos calificados son libres de impuestos. A diferencia de una Cuenta de Gastos Flexibles (FSA), que es exclusiva de empleadores y no esta disponible para propietarios unicos o freelancers sin empleo W-2, la Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud es portatil y se acumula de un ano al siguiente.

heading.es: Opcion 3: Plan del empleador del conyuge

paragraphs.en: For freelancers and independent contractors who are married to a W-2 employee with employer-sponsored health benefits, joining the spouse's plan is often the cheapest option on a total-cost basis. The spouse's employer typically subsidizes a significant portion of the family premium. Contributions come out of the W-2 spouse's paycheck pretax through a Section 125 cafeteria plan, lowering the spouse's federal income tax liability and FICA taxes simultaneously. The 1099 contractor can still file Form 7206 for any months before joining the spouse's plan during the year, but any month the contractor was eligible for the spouse's employer plan disqualifies that month from the deduction.
paragraphs.es: Para los freelancers y contratistas independientes casados con un empleado W-2 con beneficios medicos del empleador, unirse al plan del conyuge es a menudo la opcion mas economica en terminos de costo total. El empleador del conyuge tipicamente subsidia una parte significativa de la prima familiar. Las contribuciones salen del cheque de pago del conyuge W-2 antes de impuestos a traves de un plan de cafeteria de la Seccion 125, lo que reduce tanto el impuesto federal como los impuestos FICA.

heading.en: Option 4: COBRA from a Prior W-2 Job
heading.es: Opcion 4: COBRA de un empleo W-2 anterior

paragraphs.en: Contract workers who recently transitioned from W-2 employment to independent contractor status can keep their former employer's plan for up to 18 months through COBRA continuation coverage. The catch: the contractor now pays both the employee share and the employer share of the premium, plus a 2% administrative fee. A $250-per-month employee contribution can jump to $1,400 per month under COBRA. Most independent contractors find an ACA Marketplace plan cheaper within the first month. Transitioning from W-2 to self-employment is itself a qualifying life event that opens a 60-day Marketplace Special Enrollment Period, so COBRA and an ACA plan can be evaluated simultaneously before the window closes.
paragraphs.es: Los trabajadores por contrato que recientemente pasaron del empleo W-2 a la condicion de contratista independiente pueden mantener el plan de su antiguo empleador hasta 18 meses a traves de la continuacion COBRA. La trampa: el contratista ahora paga tanto la parte del empleado como la parte del empleador de la prima, mas un cargo administrativo del 2%. Pasar de W-2 a trabajo independiente es en si un evento de vida calificado que abre un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial en el Mercado de 60 dias.

intro.en: 1099 contractors and freelancers are a heavily marketed segment for substandard coverage. These products look appealing on price and damage you when you actually need care:
intro.es: Los contratistas 1099 y freelancers son un segmento muy comercializado para cobertura subestandar. Estos productos parecen atractivos en precio y le danan cuando realmente necesita atencion:

en: Not required to cover pre-existing conditions, can rescind coverage retroactively, and do not count as minimum essential coverage under the ACA. A single hospitalization can leave an independent contractor with a six-figure bill with no recourse.
es: Planes de salud de duracion limitada a corto plazo

No estan obligados a cubrir condiciones preexistentes, pueden cancelar la cobertura retroactivamente y no cuentan como cobertura esencial minima bajo el ACA. Una sola hospitalizacion puede dejar a un contratista independiente con una factura de seis cifras sin recurso.

en: Health share ministries (Medi-Share, Liberty HealthShare, Samaritan)

NOT insurance. No legal obligation to pay claims. Pre-existing conditions excluded. Lifestyle clauses covering tobacco, alcohol, mental health, and reproductive care can disqualify entire categories of treatment that a 1099 contractor needs.
es: NO son seguro. Sin obligacion legal de pagar reclamos. Condiciones preexistentes excluidas. Las clausulas de estilo de vida sobre tabaco, alcohol, salud mental y cuidado reproductivo pueden descalificar categorias enteras de tratamiento.

en: Earning $1 over 400% FPL ($63,840 single) can cost a sole proprietor $5,000 to $15,000 in lost Premium Tax Credits. Time HSA contributions, Solo 401(k) contributions, and the Form 7206 deduction to land just under the cliff if your projected MAGI is within $5,000 of the threshold.
es: Calcular mal el tope del subsidio 2026

Ganar $1 por encima del 400% FPL ($63,840 individual) puede costarle a un propietario unico $5,000 a $15,000 en Creditos Fiscales de Prima perdidos. Programe los aportes al HSA, Solo 401(k) y la deduccion del Formulario 7206 para aterrizar justo debajo del tope.

en: Claiming Form 7206 for months eligible for a spouse's employer plan

The IRS disallows the self-employed health insurance deduction for any month you or your spouse were eligible for employer-sponsored coverage. Schedule C filers who skip this rule face an audit-adjustable error and an inflated deduction claim.
es: Reclamar el Formulario 7206 en meses elegibles para el plan del conyuge

El IRS no permite la deduccion de seguro medico para autoempleados en ningun mes en que usted o su conyuge era elegible para cobertura patrocinada por el empleador. Los contribuyentes del Schedule C que omiten esta regla enfrentan un error ajustable en auditoria.

footnote.en: Verify any plan is sold on HealthCare.gov or your state exchange and covers all 10 ACA essential health benefits. Off-exchange plans that appear cheaper typically strip down coverage in ways that emerge at claim time.
footnote.es: Verifique que cualquier plan se venda en HealthCare.gov o el intercambio de su estado y cubra los 10 beneficios esenciales de salud del ACA. Los planes fuera del mercado que parecen mas baratos tipicamente reducen la cobertura de maneras que emergen al momento de reclamar.

heading.en: Premium Tax Credit (PTC) eligibility for 1099 contractors in 2026
heading.es: Elegibilidad para el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC) para contratistas 1099 en 2026

paragraphs.en: 1099 independent contractors projecting their 2026 income need to know one number: 400% of the Federal Poverty Level. For a single filer in 2026 that is $63,840; for a household of four it is $132,000. Below that line, the Premium Tax Credit (PTC) phases down as income climbs toward the 400% FPL mark. Subsidies do not snap off at 250% or 300% FPL, they shrink incrementally. At 400% FPL they stop entirely. The enhanced PTC from the Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, 2026, restoring the hard cliff. Above 400% FPL, freelancers and sole proprietors pay full unsubsidized premiums. Advance credits are paid monthly based on your projected MAGI; the IRS reconciles at tax time using the Section 1095-A form you receive from the marketplace.
paragraphs.es: Los contratistas independientes 1099 que proyectan sus ingresos 2026 necesitan conocer un numero: el 400% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza. Para un declarante individual en 2026 eso es $63,840; para un hogar de cuatro es $132,000. Por debajo de esa linea, el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC) se reduce a medida que los ingresos se acercan al 400% FPL. Los subsidios no se cortan en el 250% o 300% FPL, se reducen incrementalmente. En el 400% FPL se detienen completamente. Los creditos adelantados se pagan mensualmente basados en su MAGI proyectado; el IRS reconcilia en el momento de declarar usando el formulario Seccion 1095-A que recibe del mercado.

paragraphs.en: Contract workers and sole proprietors have more MAGI levers than W-2 employees because their deductions stack before the ACA income test. Deductible business expenses on Schedule C reduce gross 1099-NEC income to net self-employment income. Half the self-employment tax (from Schedule SE) is then deducted. The Form 7206 health insurance premium deduction further reduces MAGI. HSA contributions reduce it further still. A 1099 contractor earning $90,000 gross can plausibly project MAGI below $63,840 after stacking all four layers of deductions, qualifying for thousands in annual PTC. Document every deduction and update your marketplace income projection within 30 days of any significant income change to keep advance credits accurate and avoid a large reconciliation bill at tax time.
paragraphs.es: Los trabajadores por contrato y propietarios unicos tienen mas palancas de MAGI que los empleados W-2. Los gastos de negocio deducibles en el Schedule C reducen el ingreso bruto 1099-NEC al ingreso neto del trabajo independiente. La mitad del impuesto al trabajo independiente se deduce adicionalmente. La deduccion de primas del Formulario 7206 reduce aun mas el MAGI. Las contribuciones al HSA lo reducen aun mas. Un contratista 1099 que gana $90,000 brutos puede plausiblemente proyectar un MAGI por debajo de $63,840 despues de apilar las cuatro capas de deducciones.

list.en: 138% FPL ($22,025 single, $45,540 family of 4 in 2026): Medicaid expansion threshold in the 41 expansion states. Below this, an independent contractor typically qualifies for Medicaid rather than marketplace subsidies.
list.es: 138% FPL ($22,025 individual, $45,540 familia de 4 en 2026): umbral de expansion de Medicaid en los 41 estados de expansion. Por debajo de esto, un contratista independiente tipicamente califica para Medicaid en lugar de subsidios del mercado.

list.en: 250% FPL ($39,900 single in 2026): Top of the cost-sharing reduction (CSR) eligibility range on Silver plans. At or below 250% FPL, choosing a Silver plan unlocks lower deductibles and out-of-pocket costs in addition to the premium credit.
list.es: 250% FPL ($39,900 individual en 2026): Tope del rango de elegibilidad para reduccion de costos compartidos (CSR) en planes Silver. Al o por debajo del 250% FPL, elegir un plan Silver desbloquea deducibles mas bajos y costos de bolsillo mas bajos ademas del credito de prima.

list.en: 400% FPL ($63,840 single, $132,000 family of 4 in 2026): The subsidy cliff. At exactly 400% FPL the Premium Tax Credit reaches zero. Above this line, no marketplace subsidy is available for 2026.
list.es: 400% FPL ($63,840 individual, $132,000 familia de 4 en 2026): El tope del subsidio. En exactamente el 400% FPL, el Credito Fiscal de Prima llega a cero. Por encima de esta linea, no hay subsidio del mercado disponible para 2026.

caption.en: 2026 ACA subsidy income thresholds by household size (1099 contractors and freelancers)
caption.es: Umbrales de ingresos del subsidio ACA 2026 por tamano del hogar (contratistas 1099 y freelancers)

footnote.en: 2026 FPL guidelines per HHS ASPE. 138% FPL figures apply only in the 41 states plus DC that have adopted Medicaid expansion. In the 10 non-expansion states, the Medicaid income floor is much lower and many 1099 contractors fall into the coverage gap. 400% FPL figures are the hard subsidy cutoff for 2026 with IRA enhanced credits expired.
footnote.es: Pautas FPL 2026 segun HHS ASPE. Las cifras del 138% FPL solo aplican en los 41 estados mas DC que han adoptado la expansion de Medicaid. En los 10 estados sin expansion, el piso de ingresos de Medicaid es mucho mas bajo. Las cifras del 400% FPL son el tope estricto del subsidio para 2026 con los creditos mejorados de la IRA vencidos.

table.source: HHS ASPE 2026 Federal Poverty Guidelines, HealthCare.gov

heading.en: Self-employment health insurance deduction (Form 7206) for 1099 contractors
heading.es: Deduccion de seguro medico para autoempleados (Formulario 7206) para contratistas 1099

paragraphs.en: Form 7206 lets 1099 independent contractors, freelancers, and sole proprietors deduct 100% of health insurance premiums paid for themselves, their spouse, and their dependents as an above-the-line adjustment. The deduction flows from Form 7206 through Schedule 1 line 17 to Form 1040, reducing Adjusted Gross Income and Modified Adjusted Gross Income before the ACA subsidy income test applies. This means a Schedule C filer paying $12,000 per year in marketplace premiums can deduct the full $12,000, reducing both their taxable income and next year's advance credit calculation. Form 7206 replaced the prior self-employed health insurance worksheet inside the Schedule 1 instructions starting with tax year 2023.
paragraphs.es: El Formulario 7206 permite a los contratistas independientes 1099, freelancers y propietarios unicos deducir el 100% de las primas de seguro medico pagadas por ellos mismos, su conyuge y sus dependientes como ajuste above-the-line. La deduccion fluye del Formulario 7206 a traves del Schedule 1 linea 17 al Formulario 1040, reduciendo el Ingreso Bruto Ajustado y el Ingreso Bruto Ajustado Modificado antes de que se aplique el test de ingresos del subsidio ACA.

paragraphs.en: CRITICAL CAVEAT: Form 7206 reduces federal income tax only. The deduction does NOT reduce self-employment tax calculated on Schedule SE. Self-employment tax runs at 15.3% combined (12.4% Social Security on net earnings up to the 2026 wage base, plus 2.9% Medicare with no cap). Schedule SE calculates SE tax on net self-employment earnings before the health insurance deduction is applied. This is the most common misunderstanding among first-year 1099 contractors. A contract worker earning $80,000 net SE income owes approximately $11,304 in SE tax regardless of whether they pay $15,000 in health insurance premiums or zero. The health insurance deduction reduces the income tax bill, not the SE tax bill.
paragraphs.es: ADVERTENCIA CRITICA: El Formulario 7206 reduce el impuesto sobre la renta federal solamente. La deduccion NO reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente calculado en el Schedule SE. El impuesto al trabajo independiente corre al 15.3% combinado. El Schedule SE calcula el impuesto SE sobre las ganancias netas del trabajo independiente antes de aplicar la deduccion del seguro medico. Este es el malentendido mas comun entre los contratistas 1099 de primer ano.

paragraphs.en: Two eligibility limits apply to the Form 7206 deduction. First, the deduction cannot exceed net self-employment earnings minus half of SE tax for the year. Second, any month in which you or your spouse were eligible for employer-sponsored coverage (even if you did not enroll) disqualifies that month entirely. If a 1099 contractor was a W-2 employee through March and went independent in April, only the premiums paid April through December count. Partial-year contractors calculate the deduction month by month on Form 7206 Part I. For contractors who also have a spouse with employer coverage, confirm each month's eligibility status before calculating the deduction.
paragraphs.es: Se aplican dos limites de elegibilidad a la deduccion del Formulario 7206. Primero, la deduccion no puede exceder las ganancias netas del trabajo independiente menos la mitad del impuesto SE del ano. Segundo, cualquier mes en el que usted o su conyuge era elegible para cobertura patrocinada por el empleador descalifica ese mes completamente.

heading.en: HSA and HDHP fit for 1099 contractors in 2026
heading.es: Compatibilidad de HSA y HDHP para contratistas 1099 en 2026

paragraphs.en: A Health Savings Account (HSA) is the only triple-tax-advantaged account available in the U.S. tax code, and it requires pairing with an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). For 2026, the HDHP minimum deductible is $1,700 for self-only coverage and $3,400 for family coverage (IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19). The HDHP out-of-pocket maximum for 2026 is $8,500 self-only and $17,000 family. Once enrolled in a qualifying HDHP, a 1099 independent contractor can contribute up to $4,400 (self-only) or $8,750 (family) to an HSA in 2026, with an additional $1,000 catch-up contribution if they are 55 or older.
paragraphs.es: Una Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud (HSA) es la unica cuenta con triple ventaja fiscal disponible en el codigo tributario de EE.UU., y requiere emparejarse con un Plan con Deducible Alto (HDHP) calificado para HSA. Para 2026, el deducible minimo del HDHP es $1,700 para cobertura individual y $3,400 para cobertura familiar. Una vez inscrito en un HDHP calificado, un contratista independiente 1099 puede contribuir hasta $4,400 (individual) o $8,750 (familiar) a un HSA en 2026.

paragraphs.en: The triple tax advantage works as follows: HSA contributions are deductible above the line on Schedule 1 (reducing federal income tax AND MAGI for ACA subsidy purposes), HSA funds grow tax-free inside the account, and withdrawals for qualified medical expenses including deductibles, copays, prescriptions, dental, and vision are entirely tax-free. For a Schedule C filer in the 24% federal bracket, maxing the self-only HSA at $4,400 saves approximately $1,056 in federal income tax. For a freelancer near the 400% FPL cliff, the HSA contribution can also lower MAGI enough to restore or increase Premium Tax Credits, compounding the savings. HSA dollars roll over year to year with no use-it-or-lose-it rule and remain with the account holder even if they switch plans or return to W-2 employment.
paragraphs.es: La ventaja fiscal triple funciona asi: las contribuciones al HSA son deducibles above-the-line en el Schedule 1 (reduciendo el impuesto federal sobre la renta Y el MAGI para los subsidios ACA), los fondos del HSA crecen libres de impuestos dentro de la cuenta, y los retiros para gastos medicos calificados incluyendo deducibles, copagos, recetas, dental y vision son completamente libres de impuestos. Los dolares del HSA se acumulan de un ano al siguiente sin regla de usar o perder.

paragraphs.en: Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs) are not available to sole proprietors, freelancers, or independent contractors without W-2 employment. FSAs are set up through employer Section 125 cafeteria plans; a self-employed sole proprietor cannot open one individually. This is an important distinction: 1099 contractors often ask about FSAs after hearing about them from employed friends, but the FSA option is simply not on the table. The Health Savings Account is the correct vehicle for a contractor seeking a tax-advantaged medical savings account.
paragraphs.es: Las Cuentas de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) no estan disponibles para propietarios unicos, freelancers o contratistas independientes sin empleo W-2. Las FSA se configuran a traves de planes de cafeteria del empleador de la Seccion 125; un propietario unico autoempleado no puede abrir una individualmente. La Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud es el vehiculo correcto para un contratista que busca una cuenta de ahorros medica con ventajas fiscales.

caption.en: 2026 HSA and HDHP limits for 1099 contractors (IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19)
caption.es: Limites de HSA y HDHP 2026 para contratistas 1099 (IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19)

es: Limite de contribucion anual al HSA

footnote.en: The ACA Marketplace out-of-pocket maximum for 2026 is $10,600 individual and $21,200 family (HHS NBPP). This is higher than the HDHP OOP cap, so not every HDHP on the marketplace is HSA-qualified. Verify the plan is labeled as HSA-eligible before opening an account. FSA is employer-only and not available to sole proprietors or freelancers.
footnote.es: El maximo de bolsillo del Mercado ACA 2026 es $10,600 individual y $21,200 familiar (HHS NBPP). Esto es mas alto que el tope OOP del HDHP, por lo que no todos los HDHP del mercado califican para HSA. Verifique que el plan este etiquetado como elegible para HSA antes de abrir una cuenta. La FSA es exclusiva de empleadores y no esta disponible para propietarios unicos o freelancers.

table.source: IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19, HHS Notice of Benefit and Payment Parameters 2026

heading.es: Elegibilidad para planes catastroficos para contratistas 1099

paragraphs.en: Marketplace catastrophic plans are restricted to two groups: individuals under age 30, or individuals of any age who qualify for a hardship exemption because they cannot afford standard marketplace coverage. For a 1099 contractor who is 29 years old, a catastrophic plan carries the lowest monthly premium on the marketplace, but with a deductible equal to the 2026 ACA out-of-pocket maximum ($10,600 individual). After the deductible, most preventive services are covered at no cost. The catastrophic plan is not HSA-qualified in most cases because its deductible structure may not align precisely with IRS HDHP requirements.
paragraphs.es: Los planes catastroficos del Mercado estan restringidos a dos grupos: personas menores de 30 anos, o personas de cualquier edad que califican para una exencion de dificultades porque no pueden pagar la cobertura estandar del mercado. Para un contratista 1099 de 29 anos, un plan catastrofico tiene la prima mensual mas baja del mercado, pero con un deducible igual al maximo de bolsillo ACA 2026 ($10,600 individual).

paragraphs.en: For 1099 independent contractors and freelancers who are 30 or older, catastrophic plans are not available without a hardship exemption. The applicable hardship exemptions include: documented inability to afford coverage where the lowest available plan premium would exceed a specific percentage of household income, homelessness, domestic violence, death of a close family member, natural disaster, and a few other qualifying circumstances. A contract worker earning above 400% FPL who cannot afford full-price premiums may qualify for a hardship exemption, but this pathway is narrow and requires documentation. Most contractors 30 and older who are above the subsidy cliff are better served by an HSA-qualified Bronze HDHP than by pursuing a hardship exemption.
paragraphs.es: Para los contratistas independientes 1099 y freelancers de 30 anos o mas, los planes catastroficos no estan disponibles sin una exencion de dificultades. Las exenciones de dificultades aplicables incluyen: incapacidad documentada para pagar la cobertura, falta de vivienda, violencia domestica, muerte de un familiar cercano, desastre natural y algunas otras circunstancias calificadoras.

heading.en: Marketplace Special Enrollment Period (SEP) triggers for 1099 contractors
heading.es: Desencadenadores del Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (SEP) del Mercado para contratistas 1099

paragraphs.en: Open enrollment for 2026 ACA Marketplace plans ran from November 1, 2025 through January 15, 2026 in most states (some state exchanges extended through January 31, 2026). Outside that window, 1099 contractors and freelancers can only enroll through a Marketplace Special Enrollment Period triggered by a qualifying life event. The standard SEP window is 60 days from the date of the qualifying event. Missing the 60-day window means waiting until the next open enrollment period unless another qualifying event occurs.
paragraphs.es: La inscripcion abierta para los planes del Mercado ACA 2026 corrio desde el 1 de noviembre de 2025 hasta el 15 de enero de 2026 en la mayoria de los estados. Fuera de esa ventana, los contratistas 1099 y freelancers solo pueden inscribirse a traves de un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial del Mercado activado por un evento de vida calificador. La ventana SEP estandar es de 60 dias desde la fecha del evento calificador.

paragraphs.en: For independent contractors transitioning from W-2 employment, the most common SEP trigger is loss of minimum essential coverage. When a 1099 contractor leaves a salaried job and loses employer-sponsored health insurance, the 60-day SEP begins on the last day of employer coverage. During this window the contractor can choose an ACA Marketplace plan or evaluate COBRA side by side. Other SEP triggers relevant to contract workers and sole proprietors include gaining a dependent (birth, adoption), marriage or divorce, a permanent move to a new state, and a significant income change that crosses the Medicaid eligibility threshold (moving from above 138% FPL to below it, or vice versa). Turning 26 and aging off a parent's plan is also a qualifying event.
paragraphs.es: Para los contratistas independientes que hacen la transicion del empleo W-2, el desencadenador SEP mas comun es la perdida de cobertura esencial minima. Cuando un contratista 1099 deja un empleo asalariado y pierde el seguro medico del empleador, el SEP de 60 dias comienza en el ultimo dia de cobertura del empleador. Otros desencadenadores SEP relevantes para trabajadores por contrato incluyen ganar un dependiente, matrimonio o divorcio, mudanza permanente a un nuevo estado y un cambio significativo de ingresos que cruza el umbral de elegibilidad de Medicaid.

list.en: Loss of minimum essential coverage (leaving W-2 job, COBRA expiration, loss of parent's plan): 60-day SEP window starting from last day of prior coverage.
list.es: Perdida de cobertura esencial minima (dejar empleo W-2, expiracion de COBRA, perdida del plan de los padres): ventana SEP de 60 dias desde el ultimo dia de cobertura anterior.

list.en: Marriage or domestic partnership: 60-day SEP. Both spouses can enroll or change plans.
list.es: Matrimonio o sociedad domestica: SEP de 60 dias. Ambos conyuges pueden inscribirse o cambiar planes.

list.en: Birth, adoption, or foster placement of a child: 60-day SEP. The newborn or adopted child is added retroactively to the coverage date.
list.es: Nacimiento, adopcion o colocacion como familia de acogida de un nino: SEP de 60 dias. El nino recien nacido o adoptado se agrega retroactivamente a la fecha de cobertura.

list.en: Permanent move to a new ZIP code or state: 60-day SEP. Relevant for freelancers who relocate to access different marketplace plans or lower-premium markets.
list.es: Mudanza permanente a un nuevo codigo postal o estado: SEP de 60 dias. Relevante para freelancers que se reubican para acceder a diferentes planes del mercado.

list.en: Divorce or legal separation: 60-day SEP. A 1099 contractor who loses coverage under a spouse's employer plan upon divorce triggers this SEP.
list.es: Divorcio o separacion legal: SEP de 60 dias. Un contratista 1099 que pierde cobertura bajo el plan del empleador del conyuge al divorciarse desencadena este SEP.

list.en: Turning 26 and aging off a parent's plan: 60-day SEP from the 26th birthday. A contract worker who was on a parent's ACA plan triggers enrollment eligibility on their 26th birthday.
list.es: Cumplir 26 anos y salir del plan de los padres: SEP de 60 dias desde el 26 cumpleanos. Un trabajador por contrato que estaba en el plan ACA de los padres desencadena elegibilidad el dia de su 26 cumpleanos.

list.en: Income change crossing the Medicaid threshold: enrolling in Medicaid (if income drops below 138% FPL in expansion states) or losing Medicaid eligibility (income rises above threshold). Each is a qualifying event for marketplace enrollment.
list.es: Cambio de ingresos que cruza el umbral de Medicaid: inscribirse en Medicaid (si los ingresos caen por debajo del 138% FPL en estados de expansion) o perder la elegibilidad de Medicaid (los ingresos suben por encima del umbral). Cada uno es un evento calificador para la inscripcion en el mercado.

heading.en: How to enroll in a Marketplace plan as a 1099 contractor
heading.es: Como inscribirse en un plan del Mercado como contratista 1099

paragraphs.en: 1099 independent contractors enroll through HealthCare.gov (in federally-facilitated marketplace states) or their state's own exchange (California, New York, Massachusetts, and 14 other states run their own exchanges). The enrollment process for a sole proprietor or freelancer is similar to any individual marketplace enrollment, with one key difference: MAGI projection for self-employed income requires more careful documentation than a W-2 paycheck amount.
paragraphs.es: Los contratistas independientes 1099 se inscriben a traves de HealthCare.gov (en los estados del mercado facilitados federalmente) o el intercambio de su propio estado. El proceso de inscripcion para un propietario unico o freelancer es similar a cualquier inscripcion individual en el mercado, con una diferencia clave: la proyeccion del MAGI para el ingreso autoempleado requiere mas documentacion cuidadosa.

list.en: Step 1: Gather documents. You need your Social Security number, prior year tax return (Form 1040 with Schedule C and Schedule SE), projected current-year business income and expenses, information on any other household income, and current health insurance policy details if you have one.
list.es: Paso 1: Reunir documentos. Necesita su numero de Seguro Social, declaracion de impuestos del ano anterior (Formulario 1040 con Schedule C y Schedule SE), ingreso y gastos proyectados del negocio del ano actual, informacion sobre cualquier otro ingreso del hogar y detalles de la poliza de seguro medico actual si tiene una.

list.en: Step 2: Go to HealthCare.gov or your state exchange. Create an account or log in. Start a new application or update your existing one.
list.es: Paso 2: Ir a HealthCare.gov o el intercambio de su estado. Crear una cuenta o iniciar sesion. Iniciar una nueva solicitud o actualizar la existente.

list.en: Step 3: Enter your projected 2026 MAGI. For 1099 contractors, this means net SE income after business expenses, minus half SE tax, minus the Form 7206 health insurance deduction, minus HSA contributions, minus any retirement plan contributions. Include all household income.
list.es: Paso 3: Ingresar su MAGI 2026 proyectado. Para los contratistas 1099, esto significa el ingreso neto SE despues de los gastos del negocio, menos la mitad del impuesto SE, menos la deduccion del seguro medico del Formulario 7206, menos las contribuciones al HSA, menos las contribuciones al plan de retiro.

list.en: Step 4: Compare plans. Use the plan comparison tool to see premiums after tax credits, deductibles, out-of-pocket maximums, and network details. For contractors near the 400% FPL cliff, check whether an HDHP is HSA-qualified.
list.es: Paso 4: Comparar planes. Use la herramienta de comparacion de planes para ver primas despues de creditos fiscales, deducibles, maximos de bolsillo y detalles de la red. Para contratistas cerca del tope del 400% FPL, verifique si un HDHP es compatible con HSA.

list.en: Step 5: Complete enrollment and pay first premium. Coverage typically starts the first of the month following enrollment (or the first of the following month if you enroll after the 15th, in most cases).
list.es: Paso 5: Completar la inscripcion y pagar la primera prima. La cobertura tipicamente comienza el primero del mes siguiente a la inscripcion.

list.en: Common denial or complication reasons: inconsistent income documentation (marketplace income vs prior year tax return), failure to report household income changes within 30 days, selecting a plan outside the SEP window after a qualifying event, or attempting to enroll in a plan not available in your service area after a move.
list.es: Razones comunes de denegacion o complicacion: documentacion de ingresos inconsistente, no reportar cambios de ingresos del hogar dentro de 30 dias, seleccionar un plan fuera de la ventana SEP despues de un evento calificador, o intentar inscribirse en un plan no disponible en su area de servicio despues de una mudanza.

en.question: What is the cheapest health insurance for a 1099 contractor in 2026?
en.answer: For 1099 independent contractors with MAGI under 400% FPL ($63,840 single in 2026), an ACA Marketplace Silver plan with cost-sharing reductions is usually the cheapest after Premium Tax Credits and out-of-pocket cost reductions are counted. For contractors above 400% FPL who pay full sticker price, an HSA-qualified Bronze HDHP typically has the lowest net cost after the Health Savings Account deduction. A sole proprietor in the 24% bracket who maxes the self-only HSA at $4,400 can reduce the effective annual premium by over $1,000 in federal tax savings alone.

en.question: Do 1099 contractors qualify for the Premium Tax Credit in 2026?
en.answer: Yes, if projected MAGI is below 400% FPL. For 2026 that is $63,840 for a single filer or $132,000 for a household of four. The enhanced PTC from the Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, 2026, restoring the hard subsidy cliff. Independent contractors and freelancers with variable income can often lower MAGI below the cliff by stacking deductions: Schedule C business expenses, half of SE tax, the Form 7206 premium deduction, HSA contributions, and Solo 401(k) or SEP-IRA contributions. Section 1095-A is the form used to reconcile advance credits at tax time.

en.question: Does the Form 7206 deduction reduce self-employment tax?
en.answer: No. The self-employed health insurance deduction on Form 7206 reduces federal income tax and MAGI only. It does NOT reduce self-employment tax on Schedule SE. SE tax is 15.3% combined (12.4% Social Security plus 2.9% Medicare), calculated on net self-employment earnings before the health insurance deduction is applied. A 1099 contractor earning $70,000 net SE income owes roughly $9,890 in SE tax whether they pay $15,000 in premiums or zero. The Form 7206 deduction reduces the income tax portion of the tax bill, not the SE tax portion. This is the single most common misconception among independent contractors in their first year of self-employment.

en.question: Can a 1099 contractor use an HSA?
en.answer: Yes, as long as they are enrolled in an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). For 2026, the HDHP minimum deductible is $1,700 self-only or $3,400 family. The HSA contribution limit in 2026 is $4,400 self-only or $8,750 family, with a $1,000 catch-up if age 55 or older. Contributions are deductible above the line, reducing both income tax and MAGI. The Flexible Spending Account (FSA) is NOT available to sole proprietors or freelancers without W-2 employment; the FSA requires an employer-sponsored Section 125 plan. The Health Savings Account is the correct vehicle for a contract worker seeking a tax-advantaged medical account.

en.question: What if a freelancer earns too much for ACA subsidies in 2026?
en.answer: A freelancer or sole proprietor above 400% FPL ($63,840 single, $132,000 family of four) pays full sticker price for marketplace coverage in 2026 with no Premium Tax Credit available. The best strategy is an HSA-qualified Bronze HDHP to get the lowest premium, plus maximizing the HSA contribution ($4,400 self or $8,750 family in 2026) for the above-the-line deduction. Stacking a Solo 401(k) contribution, the Form 7206 premium deduction, and the HSA deduction can lower MAGI enough to fall below the 400% FPL cliff, potentially restoring thousands in Premium Tax Credits. Run the numbers before tax season with a CPA who handles self-employment income.

en.question: When can a 1099 contractor enroll in a Marketplace plan outside open enrollment?
en.answer: A 1099 independent contractor can enroll outside open enrollment through a Marketplace Special Enrollment Period triggered by a qualifying life event. The SEP window is 60 days from the event. Common triggers for contract workers: losing employer coverage when leaving a W-2 job, COBRA expiration, turning 26 and aging off a parent's plan, marriage, divorce, birth or adoption of a child, permanent move to a new state, and income change crossing the Medicaid eligibility threshold. Missing the 60-day SEP window means waiting for the next open enrollment (typically November 1 to January 15 each year).

en.question: Can a 1099 contractor enroll in a catastrophic plan?
en.answer: Only if under 30 years old or if they qualify for a hardship exemption. Marketplace catastrophic plans have the lowest premiums but a deductible equal to the 2026 ACA out-of-pocket maximum ($10,600 individual). For 1099 contractors 30 or older without a documented hardship, catastrophic plans are not available. Contractors 30 or older above the subsidy cliff are typically better served by an HSA-qualified Bronze HDHP, which has a lower deductible than the catastrophic plan and qualifies for the Health Savings Account triple tax advantage.

en.question: What documents does a 1099 contractor need to apply for Marketplace coverage?
en.answer: To apply on HealthCare.gov, a 1099 contractor needs: Social Security number, prior year Form 1040 with Schedule C and Schedule SE for income verification, a projected current-year MAGI estimate (gross 1099-NEC income minus business expenses, minus half SE tax, minus estimated Form 7206 premium, minus HSA and retirement contributions), any other household income information, and current insurance details if enrolling during a Special Enrollment Period. If enrolling due to loss of coverage, the coverage termination notice from the prior insurer or employer serves as SEP documentation.

es.question: ?Cual es el seguro medico mas barato para un contratista 1099 en 2026?
es.answer: Para los contratistas independientes 1099 con MAGI bajo 400% FPL ($63,840 individual en 2026), un plan Silver del Mercado ACA con reducciones de costos compartidos es usualmente el mas barato despues de los Creditos Fiscales de Prima y las reducciones de costos de bolsillo. Para los contratistas por encima del 400% FPL que pagan el precio completo, un HDHP Bronze compatible con HSA tipicamente tiene el menor costo neto despues de la deduccion de la Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud.

es.question: ?Los contratistas 1099 califican para el Credito Fiscal de Prima en 2026?
es.answer: Si, si el MAGI proyectado esta por debajo del 400% FPL. Para 2026 eso es $63,840 para un declarante individual o $132,000 para un hogar de cuatro personas. El PTC mejorado de la Ley de Reduccion de la Inflacion expiro el 1 de enero de 2026, restaurando el tope estricto del subsidio. Los contratistas y freelancers con ingresos variables a menudo pueden bajar el MAGI apilando deducciones: gastos del negocio del Schedule C, mitad del impuesto SE, la deduccion de primas del Formulario 7206, contribuciones al HSA y contribuciones al Solo 401(k) o SEP-IRA.

es.question: ?La deduccion del Formulario 7206 reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente?
es.answer: No. La deduccion de seguro medico para autoempleados del Formulario 7206 reduce el impuesto federal sobre la renta y el MAGI solamente. NO reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente en el Schedule SE. El impuesto SE es del 15.3% combinado, calculado sobre las ganancias netas del trabajo independiente antes de aplicar la deduccion del seguro medico. Este es el malentendido mas comun entre los contratistas independientes en su primer ano de trabajo por cuenta propia.

es.question: ?Puede un contratista 1099 usar una HSA?
es.answer: Si, siempre que este inscrito en un Plan con Deducible Alto (HDHP) calificado para HSA. Para 2026, el deducible minimo del HDHP es $1,700 individual o $3,400 familiar. El limite de contribucion al HSA en 2026 es $4,400 individual o $8,750 familiar, con $1,000 adicional a los 55 anos o mas. La Cuenta de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) NO esta disponible para propietarios unicos o freelancers sin empleo W-2; la FSA requiere un plan de cafeteria de la Seccion 125 del empleador. La Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud es el vehiculo correcto para un trabajador por contrato.

es.question: ?Que pasa si un freelancer gana demasiado para los subsidios ACA en 2026?
es.answer: Un freelancer o propietario unico por encima del 400% FPL ($63,840 individual, $132,000 familia de cuatro) paga el precio completo por la cobertura del mercado en 2026 sin Credito Fiscal de Prima disponible. La mejor estrategia es un HDHP Bronze compatible con HSA para obtener la prima mas baja, mas maximizar la contribucion al HSA ($4,400 individual o $8,750 familiar en 2026). Apilar una contribucion al Solo 401(k), la deduccion de primas del Formulario 7206 y la deduccion del HSA puede bajar el MAGI suficientemente para caer por debajo del tope del 400% FPL.

es.question: ?Cuando puede un contratista 1099 inscribirse en un plan del Mercado fuera de la inscripcion abierta?
es.answer: Un contratista independiente 1099 puede inscribirse fuera de la inscripcion abierta a traves de un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial del Mercado activado por un evento de vida calificador. La ventana SEP es de 60 dias desde el evento. Desencadenadores comunes para trabajadores por contrato: perder la cobertura del empleador al dejar un empleo W-2, expiracion de COBRA, cumplir 26 anos y salir del plan de los padres, matrimonio, divorcio, nacimiento o adopcion de un nino, mudanza permanente a un nuevo estado y cambio de ingresos que cruza el umbral de elegibilidad de Medicaid.

es.question: ?Puede un contratista 1099 inscribirse en un plan catastrofico?
es.answer: Solo si tiene menos de 30 anos o si califica para una exencion de dificultades. Los planes catastroficos del Mercado tienen las primas mas bajas pero un deducible igual al maximo de bolsillo ACA 2026 ($10,600 individual). Para los contratistas 1099 de 30 anos o mas sin una dificultad documentada, los planes catastroficos no estan disponibles. Los contratistas de 30 anos o mas por encima del tope del subsidio tipicamente se benefician mas de un HDHP Bronze compatible con HSA.

es.question: ?Que documentos necesita un contratista 1099 para solicitar cobertura del Mercado?
es.answer: Para solicitar en HealthCare.gov, un contratista 1099 necesita: numero de Seguro Social, Formulario 1040 del ano anterior con Schedule C y Schedule SE, una estimacion del MAGI del ano actual proyectado (ingreso bruto 1099-NEC menos gastos del negocio, menos la mitad del impuesto SE, menos la prima estimada del Formulario 7206, menos las contribuciones al HSA y plan de retiro), cualquier otro ingreso del hogar y detalles del seguro actual si se inscribe durante un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial.

note.en: Form 7206 and instructions for the 100% above-the-line premium deduction. Confirms deduction reduces income tax only, not SE tax.
note.es: Formulario 7206 e instrucciones para la deduccion del 100% above-the-line. Confirma que la deduccion reduce el impuesto sobre la renta solamente, no el impuesto SE.

note.en: Schedule SE instructions confirm the 15.3% SE tax is calculated before the health insurance deduction applies.
note.es: Las instrucciones del Schedule SE confirman que el 15.3% del impuesto SE se calcula antes de aplicar la deduccion del seguro medico.
